summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a classic ’73 show – crisp mix, tight band and a legendary Playing‑in‑the‑Band sandwich with El Paso, Dark Star jam, Wharf Rat and Morning Dew.
review: The Charlie Miller remaster delivers crystal‑clear soundboard quality that fans repeatedly call “crispy” and “well‑balanced.”  Musically the performance is a high point of the 1973 era – the rain‑themed first set flows into an inventive second‑set sandwich (Half Step → Playing → El Paso → Playing → Dark Star jam → Wharf Rat → Playing → Morning Dew) that many reviewers cite as a top‑tier moment, with standout renditions of Box of Rain, Here Comes Sunshine and Looks Like Rain.  A few dissenters note the El Paso insert feels unnecessary, but overall sentiment is overwhelmingly positive.

ai_rating.rationale: More than a dozen detailed 5‑star reviews praise both the musical performance and the soundboard quality; only one low‑rating comment is unrelated to the show itself.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced mix; Jerry’s guitar, Bobby’s phasing on Wharf Rat and Keith’s keyboards sit nicely.  Some note the vocals are a touch low and Phil’s bass was pulled back due to Coliseum acoustics, but overall the remaster is described as one of the best on the Archive.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A rain‑themed first set (Box of Rain → Here Comes Sunshine → Looks Like Rain) segues into a uniquely creative second‑set sandwich that many fans call one of the era’s best.  The El Paso insertion divides opinion, but most feel it adds to the adventurous flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Blinding feedback tone in the Playin' jam, “melt‑face” guitar solos, praised as beardless and energetic.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass a bit subdued due to venue acoustics; otherwise solid foundation.
band_member_comments.Bob: Great phasing on Wharf Rat, steady rhythm work throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s contributions highlighted in jams (especially during the Playing sandwich) and noted as strong.
band_member_comments.Drums: Tight overall drumming; no specific complaints.
